channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Wed, 08 Jul 2026 05:18:14 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: What happened to Sur Ronster? Team shares update after e-bike YouTuber’s shocking Ultra Bee crash
item.description: Sur Ronster is recovering after what his team called the worst crash of his career while testing a Sur Ron Ultra Bee e-bike. The update came through his official TikTok account, which confirmed he can read fans’ comments while recovering. No injury details or return date have been released. The news comes months after his Sydney Harbour Bridge controversy.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Mizkif releases “Emiru Lied, Part 1” video presenting evidence amid defamation lawsuit
item.description: Streamer Mizkif shared his side of a legal dispute with Emiru. He presented messages and conversations to support his claims in the lawsuit. Mizkif addressed allegations about their past relationship and subsequent interactions. He also discussed claims of him trying to follow Emiru after their breakup. The legal battle between the two streamers continues with new information emerging.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Kai Cenat’s first stream in nine months tops 1 million viewers with Streamer University 2026 reveal
item.description: Kai Cenat returned to livestreaming after a nine-month break, drawing millions of excited fans. His comeback stream simultaneously broadcast on Twitch and YouTube, a first for the popular streamer. The event successfully revealed the professors and students for the upcoming Streamer University 2026 program. Over one million viewers tuned in live across both platforms, showcasing his immense popularity.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: ‘Streaming makes me the worst’: JiDion quits Kick streaming after viral arrest forces major self-reflection
item.description: JiDion, whose real name is Jidon Armani Adams, says he is quitting streaming after his June 22 arrest outside a McDonald’s in Woodhaven, Michigan. In a new YouTube video, he called the incident a wake-up call and said streaming brought out the worst in him. Adams plans to stop livestreaming and focus on predator-catching content instead.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Indian-origin motel manager arrested in Los Angeles for profiting off prostitution, sex-trafficking
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 2018 murder of newborn in California: Bakshinderpal Sing Mann, father who buried baby and then fled to Canada, finally sentenced
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK govt mulls emergency law to deport grooming gang ringleader to Pakistan
item.description: Labour is exploring emergency legislation to deport a grooming gang leader. Shabir Ahmed, convicted of child rape, was released after serving fourteen years. He is currently exempt from deportation due to an old immigration law. The government is considering legislative changes to address this loophole. Discussions are ongoing with Pakistan to facilitate his removal from the UK.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Canton

item.title: Quote of the day by Michelle Obama: 'Failure is a feeling long before it becomes an actual result'
item.description: Quote of the day by Michelle Obama: In her 2018 memoir 'Becoming', Michelle Obama wrote about her childhood in Chicago. Her school Bryn Mawr was at the heart of her childhood and Michelle Obama's famous quote on failure came in the context of a crisis that her school was facing and how the principal resisted it.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'She's a nice person but made a mistake': Trump says ties with Meloni 'soured' over Iran war
item.description: US President Donald Trump described Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni as nice but criticized her stance. He stated her refusal to support the US in the Iran conflict soured their relationship. This dispute follows earlier claims by Trump about Meloni requesting a photograph. The Italian government has decided to stop responding to Trump's provocative social media remarks. Meloni's office also declined comment on their upcoming meeting at the NATO summit.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Have you ever faced harm or mistreatment in India? Why US visa officers ask this question to every candidate
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Manhattan high-rise evacuated as officials warn of potential collapse
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Giorgia Meloni: 'If I were fascist, I would say that I am fascist. I never hide'
item.description: Quote of the day by Giorgia Meloni: Before becoming the PM of Italy, Meloni directly addressed a question on whether she was a fascist leader. She insisted that when she co-founded her party Brothers of Italy in 2012, the party was 'centre-right' and not fascist. "When I am something, I declare it. I never hide. If I were fascist, I would say that I am fascist. Instead, I have never spoken of fascism because I am not fascist," Meloni said.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Third tanker hit in Strait of Hormuz in 24 hours as Qatar blames Iran, raising fears over global energy route
item.description: Commercial tankers faced escalating attacks in the Strait of Hormuz recently. Qatar blamed Iran for an unacceptable assault on its liquefied natural gas vessel. The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations reported three tanker incidents within 24 hours. These attacks threaten regional stability and global energy security significantly. Diplomatic efforts continue to prevent further escalation of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: TOI News Desk

item.title: What is E-2 visa? Immigration attorney says you don't need to start a business from scratch
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Candace Owens-Andrew Kolvet feud reaches boiling point over Charlie Kirk legacy fight: ‘You are a CLOWN’
item.description: Candace Owens and Andrew Kolvet are locked in a public feud over Charlie Kirk’s legacy and the nature of Owens’ friendship with the late TPUSA founder. After Kolvet suggested Kirk was merely “placating” her, Owens responded with a lengthy X post, calling him a “CLOWN” and a liar. The dispute has widened existing divisions within conservative circles.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Trump announces Turkey sanctions lift at Nato summit, signals possible F-35 fighter jet sale
item.description: US President Donald Trump has taken a notable step by removing sanctions on Turkey and hinted at the sale of F-35 fighter jets to the nation. This move follows Turkey's acquisition of Russian air defense systems, which had led to its exclusion from the F-35 program. Trump praised Turkey for its commitment to U.S. relations, showcasing a potential realignment in diplomatic ties.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: France's Marine Le Pen sentenced to house arrest, 2027 presidential bid remains uncertain
item.description: Marine Le Pen faces house arrest and a ban from public office after a court ruling. This sentence could impact her presidential election bid scheduled for April and May 2027. The case involves allegations of misusing European Parliament funds for fake jobs between 2004 and 2016. Le Pen denies the charges and claims the party acted in good faith.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists tried to thicken Arctic ice by pumping seawater onto it. The ice did become thicker and brighter, but there is a big catch
item.description: Scientists tested spraying seawater onto Arctic ice, which showed promising initial results. The treated ice became thicker and more reflective, resisting summer melting effectively. This method offers an alternative to controversial geoengineering approaches for ice preservation. While promising, researchers note significant challenges for large-scale implementation across the Arctic. Further work is essential before this technique can be considered for broader application.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: $88M LA mansion, even JLo eyed, sells for half its price
item.description: The Jennifer Lopez-admired Holmby Hills estate is now under contract after many years. This lavish property, formerly owned by Max Azria, had a steep price reduction. Jennifer Lopez had previously considered purchasing the grand estate on two separate occasions. The 2.8-acre compound features extensive gardens and a glass-enclosed tennis court. The deal has not yet closed, and the final sale price remains confidential.
item.dc:creator: TOI Real Estate Desk

item.title: Can Satellites fight? Space force stages orbital war game between two spacecraft
item.description: Space Force recently tested satellite interception capabilities in orbit. Two commercial satellites successfully located and assessed each other during the mission. This exercise demonstrated rapid identification and inspection of other spacecraft. The US Space Force is preparing for potential future conflicts in space. Such missions are becoming crucial for broader defense planning and strategic competition.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
